questions.question_text: How many licensed real estate brokers serve on the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: PREC has 11 members total, including 5 licensed real estate brokers who must each have at least 10 years of licensure at appointment. The remaining seats go to 3 public members, 1 cemetery broker, and 2 ex officio members.

questions.question_text: A broker nominated to the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission must have been licensed for at least how many years?
questions.explanation: Each of the 5 broker members must have been licensed as a real estate broker for at least 10 years at the time of appointment. The cemetery broker member has a separate requirement of 5 years licensure plus 10 years selling cemetery lots.

questions.question_text: Who confirms appointments to the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: All PREC members are appointed by the Governor and confirmed by the Pennsylvania Senate. Members serve 4-year terms.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an ex officio member of the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: The two ex officio PREC members are the Commissioner of the Bureau of Professional and Occupational Affairs (BPOA) and the Director of the Bureau of Consumer Protection (or designee).

questions.question_text: The Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission includes how many public members who are NOT licensed in real estate?
questions.explanation: PREC's 11-member composition includes 3 public members who are not licensed in any real estate capacity. This ensures consumer perspectives are represented alongside the 5 brokers, 1 cemetery broker, and 2 ex officio members.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum civil penalty PREC may impose per violation of the Real Estate Licensing and Registration Act?
questions.explanation: Under RELRA, PREC can levy civil penalties up to $1,000 per violation. This is in addition to other disciplinary actions like license suspension or revocation.

questions.question_text: PREC may impose which of the following as a disciplinary action?
distractors: Permanent industry ban with no reapplication
questions.explanation: PREC's disciplinary options include fines up to $1,000 per violation, probation with conditions, license suspension, and license revocation. Criminal penalties and permanent bans are not within PREC's authority.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a power of the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: PREC can license, discipline, regulate, and investigate, but criminal prosecution is handled by the Attorney General or district attorneys. PREC's enforcement is administrative and civil in nature.

questions.question_text: PREC approves which of the following for real estate education providers?
questions.explanation: PREC has the authority to approve pre-license schools and courses, as well as set and enforce continuing education requirements. This covers both salesperson and broker education programs across all delivery formats.

questions.question_text: PREC members serve terms of how many years?
questions.explanation: All PREC members are appointed by the Governor, confirmed by the Senate, and serve 4-year terms. This applies to both the broker members and the public members.

questions.question_text: What is the official abbreviation of the statute governing Pennsylvania real estate licensing?
questions.explanation: RELRA stands for the Real Estate Licensing and Registration Act (63 P.S. § 455.101 et seq.), enacted as Act 9 of 1980. It is the primary statute governing real estate licensure in Pennsylvania.

questions.question_text: PREC operates under which state bureau within the Department of State?
questions.correct_answer: Bureau of Professional and Occupational Affairs
questions.explanation: PREC operates under the Bureau of Professional and Occupational Affairs (BPOA), which is part of the Pennsylvania Department of State. BPOA oversees all professional licensing boards in the commonwealth.

questions.question_text: RELRA grants PREC authority over all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: RELRA gives PREC broad authority over licensing, education, investigation, and discipline. However, commission rates are freely negotiable and PREC has no authority to set or cap them.

questions.question_text: A licensee asks which law requires them to provide a Consumer Notice form. The correct answer is:
questions.explanation: The Consumer Notice requirement comes from RELRA and 49 Pa. Code Chapter 35, not from federal law or the PHRA. RELRA governs all aspects of real estate licensee conduct in Pennsylvania.

questions.question_text: A licensee violates a PREC regulation but claims they didn't know the rule existed. This defense is:
questions.correct_answer: Not valid — licensees must know applicable laws
distractors: Valid if the broker also didn't know
Valid if the rule was adopted within the past year
questions.explanation: Ignorance of RELRA or PREC regulations is not a defense. Licensees are expected to know and comply with all applicable laws and regulations governing their practice.
